summary: PR 2739 reorganized the former gm_1A4C slice into gm_1A7A and gmregenddisp units, adjusted splits/configure entries, changed many gm .sdata2 symbols from global to local scope, and added source/header updates that improved reported GALE01 data matching by +0.25% (+3080 bytes).

key_files.role: Split former melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c range into melee/gm/gm_1A7A.c and melee/gm/gmregenddisp.c with separate text/data/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:splits.txt gm_1A4C.c removed; gm_1A7A.c and gmregenddisp.c ranges added

key_files.role: Changed gm_804DAA00 through gm_804DAAA4 .sdata2 symbol scope from global to local, with two 8-byte entries marked data:float after the change.
Updated build object list to replace gm_1A4C.c with gm_1A7A.c and gmregenddisp.c, both NonMatching.
New source file containing early 0x801A7A44-0x801A7FB4 functions including animation callbacks and gm_801A7B00 setup code.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report:new matches and near-match for main/melee/gm/gm_1A7A

key_files.role: Renamed from gm_1A4C.h to gm_1A7A.h, added includes and two inline helper candidates with a TODO about placement.
Updated aggregate gm include export from gm_1A4C.h to gm_1A7A.h.
Renamed from gm_1A4C.c and retained the later 0x801A7FB4-0x801A9DD0 registration/end-display code after moving earlier functions to gm_1A7A.c.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report:new matches for main/melee/gm/gmregenddisp

key_files.role: Changed by one line according to metadata, but loaded diff excerpt does not show the hunk.

what_changed: Replaced the single gm_1A4C split with two object units: gm_1A7A and gmregenddisp.
Moved functions fn_801A7A44, fn_801A7A68, fn_801A7A8C, and gm_801A7B00 into new src/melee/gm/gm_1A7A.c.
Renamed gm_1A4C.c to gmregenddisp.c and removed the early 0x801A7A44-era functions from that file.
Renamed gm_1A4C.h to gm_1A7A.h and updated gm_unsplit.h to export the new header.
Adjusted configure.py to list gm_1A7A.c and gmregenddisp.c as separate NonMatching objects.
Changed a block of gm_804DAAxx .sdata2 symbols from global to local scope in symbols.txt.
Bot report recorded 21 new matches, including gmregenddisp .data/.bss/.sdata/.sdata2 and multiple gmregenddisp/gm_1A7A functions, plus improvements in unmatched gmregenddisp and gm_1A7A functions.

smart_moves.description: Separated a large gm source/split into two more precise units, aligning config split ranges with new source file boundaries.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report:new matches under gmregenddisp and gm_1A7A

smart_moves.description: Made local .sdata2 ownership explicit by changing gm_804DAA00-gm_804DAAA4 symbols from global to local scope.
Kept the renamed header wired through gm_unsplit.h so existing unsplit include consumers follow the renamed gm_1A7A header.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a source file covers multiple natural split ranges, splitting the TU and updating splits/configure can turn apparent broken/partial data matches into per-unit matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report:gmregenddisp and gm_1A7A new matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: For data-match work, symbol scope in symbols.txt matters: changing address-style .sdata2 symbols from global to local can be part of section ownership cleanup when the split boundaries justify local ownership.
Header renames caused by split renames need corresponding aggregate include updates, as shown by gm_unsplit.h switching from gm_1A4C.h to gm_1A7A.h.

naming_conventions.name: gm_1A7A
naming_conventions.convention: Address-range style file/header name for the new early gm slice beginning at 0x801A7A44.

naming_conventions.name: gmregenddisp
naming_conventions.convention: Semantic file name used for the later renamed gm display/registration-end-display slice formerly covered by gm_1A4C.c.

naming_conventions.name: gm_804DAAxx
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for sdata2 objects while scope metadata changed from global to local; the PR does not provide semantic rename evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Unit-boundary retargeting: replace one larger object with two smaller objects in splits.txt and configure.py to improve data/function match accounting.
Localize data symbols when their ownership belongs to the new split unit instead of exposing them as globals.
A narrow PAD_STACK(0x30) appears in gm_801A7B00; the bot report shows gm_801A7B00 improved to 98.87%, but the PR slice does not include human discussion justifying the padding.
Two near-identical inline helpers were added in gm_1A7A.h with a TODO about placement, suggesting temporary helper-boundary experimentation rather than settled API ownership.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report recorded matched data at 48.48%, +0.25%, +3080 bytes, with 21 new matches and several unmatched-item improvements. The report also lists broken/regressed items under the old gm_1A4C unit, consistent with the unit being replaced by gm_1A7A and gmregenddisp.

evidence_refs: diff_excerpt for splits.txt symbols.txt configure.py gm_1A7A.c gm_1A7A.h gm_unsplit.h gmregenddisp.c
notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The diff excerpt/raw diff is truncated, so gmregenddisp.c and lbbgflash.c details are incomplete.
The bot report provides match-result evidence, but no build command or human explanation of author intent.
PAD_STACK and inline helper placement appear in the diff, but the slice lacks review discussion proving they are accepted style beyond this PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Splitting a broad object into smaller split-aligned units can improve data matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2739 replaced the former gm_1A4C unit with gm_1A7A and gmregenddisp split/configure entries. The bot report then showed new matches for gmregenddisp and gm_1A7A sections/functions and +3080 matched data bytes. Treat as a data-match tactic candidate, not a universal rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Retain address-style names when only ownership/scope changes
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR changed gm_804DAA00-gm_804DAAA4 from global to local scope while keeping address-style names, supporting conservative naming when semantic meaning is not evidenced.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1A7A/gmregenddisp split introduced from former gm_1A4C range
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2739 split the former melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c range into melee/gm/gm_1A7A.c at text 0x801A7A44-0x801A7FB4 and melee/gm/gmregenddisp.c at text 0x801A7FB4-0x801A9DD0, with corresponding data/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future gm path lookups understand why gm_1A4C disappears and gm_1A7A/gmregenddisp appear.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_804DAA00-gm_804DAAA4 local sdata2 ownership
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2739 changed gm_804DAA00 through gm_804DAAA4 .sdata2 symbol metadata from scope:global to scope:local in config/GALE01/symbols.txt during the gm_1A7A/gmregenddisp split.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as path-scoped data ownership context, but should be verified against current symbols before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate temporary inline helper placement in gm_1A7A.h
source_update_candidates.text: gm_1A7A.h added fn_801A7FB4_inline and fn_801A7FB4_inline2 with a TODO: 'Figure out where this goes'. This appears to be unsettled helper/API placement rather than a confirmed convention.
source_update_candidates.reason: Route to curator/source review as a possible cleanup or header-ownership note, not as an accepted standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all gm_804DAAxx symbols should be local solely from this PR; the evidence covers only the listed range and this split.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(0x30) in gm_801A7B00 as general style; it is a local matching tactic with limited bot-report evidence.
Do not claim human reviewer approval or rationale; no human review comments are present in the loaded slice.
Do not infer lbbgflash.c details beyond the one-line changed-file metadata because the loaded diff excerpt does not show that hunk.
